THE MOTION IS ACTUALLY BEING DECIDED BY JUDGE RICHARD ULMER, SO THE MOTION FOR DISQUALIFICATION IS MOOT. JUDGE RICHARD ULMER. NOT REPORTED.

Notice And Motion To Compel Compliance With Inspection Demand And Production Of Documents And For Reasonable Atty'S Fees And Expenses; No Separate Statement Required; Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Feb-18-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, tentative ruling is adopted. DEFENDANT MATEO TOSBATH, M.D.'s Motion To Compel Compliance With Inspection Demand And Production Of Documents.
(C) The Motion to Compel Compliance with Inspection Demands is DENIED. Plaintiff's counsel has stated that she "offered to copy the documents for defendant Tosbath at an estimated cost of $200 or to produce the documents at either defendant Tosbath's offices or at hers." This is reasonable. All requests for sanctions are also DENIED. Judge Richard Ulmer. Not reported.

Motice For Disqualification Of Commisssioner Judge Pro Tem Everett A. Hewett, Jr., From Hearing Any And All Matters In The Case;
Off Calendar Feb-18-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, tentative ruling is adopted. DEFENDANT MATEO TOSBATH, M.D.'s Motion For Disqualification Of Commisssioner Judge Pro Tem Everett A. Hewett, Jr., From Hearing Any And All Matters In The Case.
(A) Defendant's Motion for Disqualification is premised on the notion that Commissioner Hewlett "has pending before him a motion to compel further answers to inspection demands." The motion is actually being decided by Judge Richard Ulmer, so the motion for disqualification is MOOT. Judge Richard Ulmer. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Aswers To Inspection Demands And For Reasonable Attorney'S Fees And Expenses;Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Feb-18-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, tentative ruling is adopted. DEFENDANT MATEO TOSBATH, M.D. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Aswers To Inspection Demands And For Reasonable Attorney'S Fees And Expenses.
(B) The Motion to Compel Further Answers is DENIED as to Inspection Demand 14, because the demand is vague, ambiguous and/or unintelligible, and DENIED as to Inspection Demands 15 and 17 for failure to comply with the California Rules of Court, including but not limited to CRC 3.1345(c)(5). All requests for sanctions are also DENIED. Judge Richard Ulmer. Not reported.
